title: Makefile: use :Z for volume mount (SELinux)
body: As per http://www.projectatomic.io/blog/2015/06/using-volumes-with-docker-can-cause-problems-with-selinux/, use `:Z` to volume mount. Without this, make fails with permission issue on SELinux enabled host.

comments.body: Ping @duglin does docker have this selinux label also?
(I am, philosophically, a bit unhappy with a simple `make all` changing context of files in my home directory. But then `--security-opt label:disable` is not better, so I don’t have any better idea.)

You could force a relabel back to the correct label in after the docker run.

restorecon -F-R ${PWD}
